title: CBSE vs student: Board admits flaws after teen hacker exposes website vulnerabilities
content: CBSE has addressed security vulnerabilities found in its OnMark portal, thanking ethical hackers for their assistance. A cybersecurity team, including experts from government agencies and IITs, has been deployed to strengthen the system. The statement comes after 19-year-old ethical hacker Nisarga Adhikary alleged security flaws in CBSE's digital evaluation ecosystem

title: IPL Final: Home advantage or batting depth — where the title may ultimately be decided
content: In a nail-biting conclusion to IPL 2026, the Royal Challengers Bengaluru face off against the formidable Gujarat Titans, two teams that have dominated the season with their impressive performances. With top-order hitters ready to set the stage ablaze and fast bowlers eager to claim wickets, the final at Ahmedabad promises a spectacle.

title: UP gets permanent DGP after 4 years, Rajeev Krishna appointed state police chief
content: Rajeev Krishna, a 1991 batch IPS officer, has been appointed as the new Director General of Police (DGP) of Uttar Pradesh, ending years of uncertainty. Krishna, who previously served as acting DGP, will lead the state's police force with nearly three years of service remaining. His appointment signals a focus on technology-led policing reforms and strengthening operational effectiveness.

title: JP Morgan CEO vows to fight against Act disputed by banking and crypto industries
content: JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon has fiercely criticized Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong and vowed to oppose the Clarity Act, a digital-asset bill advancing in Congress. Dimon dismissed Armstrong's stance and stated banks will not accept the current legislation, which centers on stablecoin interest payments. Banks argue these yields lack regulatory safeguards, while crypto firms see them as customer rewards.

title: Stock market recap: Seven of top-10 firms lose Rs 1.54 lakh crore
content: Seven of India's top 10 companies experienced a combined market valuation drop of Rs 1.54 lakh crore last week, with Reliance Industries leading the decline. This occurred as the BSE Sensex and NSE Nifty saw minor dips. Despite the losses, Reliance Industries maintained its position as the country's most-valued firm.

title: 'Amazon would collapse if run like New York City': Jeff Bezos takes swipe at Mamdani
content: Amazon founder Jeff Bezos has endorsed New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani's new government efficiency initiative, COGE. This comes after their public disagreement on taxing the wealthy. Bezos, who previously criticized city spending, now supports Mamdani's plan to make government work smarter, suggesting savings could benefit lower earners.

title: Rajat Patidar: The injury replacement turned captain who changed the fate of RCB
content: Soft-spoken away from the spotlight and measured in his demeanour, Rajat Patidar never fit the image of a franchise-defining superstar. Yet the injury replacement who once went unsold at auction became the captain who delivered RCB's maiden IPL title. Now, after another evolution in his leadership and batting, Patidar stands one victory away from achieving what only Rohit Sharma and MS Dhoni have managed.

title: Middle East conflict is rewriting your kitchen math — Explained in 10 charts
content: Geopolitical tensions in the Middle East are directly impacting household budgets, driving up prices for everyday essentials like cooking oil, milk, and bread. Soaring global crude oil prices and a weakening rupee are increasing import, fuel, and transportation costs, leading to a domino effect on consumer goods and even healthcare services.

title: Hundreds of faculty members of University of California sign open letter
content: University of California faculty are urging the reinstatement of SAT/ACT math requirements for STEM applicants, citing a significant decline in student math skills since the tests were dropped. They argue current admissions practices fail to ensure readiness, forcing instructors to reteach basic math and jeopardizing the quality of STEM education.

title: At least 5 killed after collapse at illegal mining operation in China
content: A collapse at an illegal mining operation in China's Yunnan province on Sunday resulted in at least five fatalities and one injury. Six individuals were trapped, with all recovered and taken to the hospital. Authorities are investigating the cause of the incident, which follows a deadly coal mine explosion in Shanxi province.

title: 'A chat with Soros agents': Rahul's dig at BJP as he meets CBSE students
content: Rahul Gandhi met CBSE Class 12 students labelled 'anti-national' after questioning an incorrect Physics answer sheet. The board later admitted an error. Gandhi criticised the government's response, stating the students deserve a secure future and that problems can't be solved by denying them. The incident highlighted scrutiny of the board's evaluation process.

title: 'Began firing indiscriminately': How UP police gunned down key accused in Ghaziabad teen murder
content: A 17-year-old murder suspect, Asad, carrying a Rs 50,000 reward, was killed in a police encounter in Ghaziabad's Khoda Colony. Asad was wanted for the stabbing death of a teenager earlier this week. During a chase, Asad and an associate opened fire on police, leading to a shootout where Asad was fatally injured. A constable was also wounded.

title: India-US trade talks to resume on June 1 as both sides push to finalise interim pact
content: Senior officials from India and the US are meeting in New Delhi to finalize an interim trade agreement and advance broader bilateral trade talks. Discussions will cover market access, customs, and investment, with potential recalibration of the deal due to recent US tariff changes. The agreement aims to boost trade and economic security between the two nations.

title: Tendulkar's major IPL suggestion: Scrap Impact Player rule, allow 5 overs per bowler
content: Cricket legend Sachin Tendulkar has advocated for significant IPL rule changes, including the removal of the Impact Player rule, which he believes heavily favors batters. Tendulkar also proposed a bowling powerplay and allowing one bowler to deliver five overs to restore game balance.

title: Who replaces DKS as Karnataka Congress chief? Kharge says search is on
content: Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge stated that the party is actively searching for a new Karnataka Pradesh Congress Committee (KPCC) president to strengthen the organization for upcoming elections. Discussions regarding the new Karnataka cabinet composition are ongoing, with a phased expansion potentially occurring after the June 3 swearing-in ceremony.

title: Dalal Street outlook: What will guide market sentiments this week?
content: Dalal Street anticipates a busy week driven by the RBI's interest rate decision, key economic data, and global developments like the US-Iran situation and crude oil prices. Investors will closely track manufacturing and services PMI, GDP growth, and foreign exchange reserves for market direction.

title: With assets worth Rs 1,413 crore, DK Shivakumar set to become India's richest CM
content: Karnataka's D K Shivakumar has become the wealthiest chief minister in India, with declared assets totaling Rs 1,413 crore. His wealth has significantly increased since 2008, largely due to inherited land and property appreciation. Shivakumar's assets include substantial immovable and movable properties, with liabilities of Rs 265 crore.

title: As ACs run nonstop in 45°C heat, are Delhi homes facing a growing fire hazard?
content: Delhi-NCR's sweltering heat is pushing ACs to their limits, sparking a rise in dangerous fires. Faulty wiring, overloaded circuits, and poor maintenance are increasingly causing AC-related blazes, with recent fatal incidents highlighting the urgent need for awareness. Experts warn that ignoring early warning signs like burning smells or flickering lights can have devastating consequences.

title: FPI profile: Foreign investors continue selling spree in May, pull out Rs 32,000 crore
content: Foreign portfolio investors continued their exit from Indian equities in May, withdrawing Rs 32,963 crore due to concerns over earnings growth and a weakening rupee. This outflow has pushed cumulative FPI withdrawals in 2026 to Rs 2.25 lakh crore, surpassing the entire previous year's figure. While selling intensity has eased, a turnaround in flows is unlikely without significant macroeconomic improvement.

title: 4 dead in south Delhi building collapse
content: A four-storey building collapsed near Saket Metro Station in south Delhi, killing at least four people and injuring several others. Rescue operations are underway with fears that five to six more individuals may be trapped under the debris. The building, reportedly undergoing additional construction, collapsed onto an adjacent canteen.

title: 'I was hit on head, left bleeding': After Abhishek, TMC MP Kalyan Banerjee attacked in Hooghly
content: TMC leader Kalyan Banerjee alleged he was attacked by BJP workers near Chanditala Police Station in West Bengal's Hooghly district while submitting a deputation on post-poll violence. Banerjee claimed he was hit on the head with a stone and accused the police of inaction, calling it an attempt to murder.

title: Gunfire in Himachal's Kasol: Tourist shoots local youth after argument, video goes viral
content: Four tourists from Punjab were arrested and a fifth is being sought after a local youth was shot in the leg during a violent altercation in Kasol. The incident, which occurred Saturday evening, involved a dispute escalating into a physical fight where a pistol was allegedly fired. The injured youth is out of danger, and legal proceedings have begun.

title: From heatwave precautions to community service: PM Modi's top quotes from Mann Ki Baat
content: Prime Minister Modi's Mann Ki Baat highlighted inspiring social service, water conservation, and sports achievements. He celebrated India's mango heritage, urged heatwave precautions, and lauded traditional summer drinks reflecting cultural diversity. The address emphasized citizen participation and recognizing ordinary Indians making extraordinary contributions to nation-building.

title: Israel captures strategic Lebanon castle in deepest incursion in 26 years
content: Israeli troops have captured the strategic Beaufort Castle in southern Lebanon, marking their deepest incursion in over 26 years. This territorial gain occurred despite an existing ceasefire, with forces advancing near Nabatiyeh to dismantle Hezbollah infrastructure. The operation aims to eliminate perceived threats to Israeli civilians.

title: Who is Rounak Adhikary? The entrepreneur living every startup founder's dream
content: Dismissed by Ashneer Grover with a curt 'Tu baith jaa yaar' four years ago, Rounak Adhikary's startup, ProjectX, has now been accepted into the prestigious Y Combinator accelerator. Overcoming over 200 rejections and personal bankruptcy, Adhikary's journey, shaped by childhood bullying and a slow computer, highlights resilience and unwavering conviction in pursuing ambitious dreams against all odds.

title: 'I'm worried about ... ': De Villiers' big concern for RCB ahead of IPL final vs GT
content: Despite Royal Challengers Bengaluru's dominant Qualifier 1 win, former star AB de Villiers expressed concern over Gujarat Titans' formidable bowling attack heading into the IPL 2026 final. Both teams are chasing their second IPL crown, with RCB aiming to defend their title and GT seeking to add to their 2022 victory.

title: Watch: Russia's 'fire-and-forget' $500 Yolka drone hunter that fits in soldier's hand
content: New footage shows Russian forces deploying the "Yolka" interceptor drone system in combat, a low-cost weapon designed to counter enemy reconnaissance and FPV drones. This handheld system launches a drone that autonomously tracks and engages targets via kinetic impact, offering a portable and cost-effective solution against battlefield UAVs.

title: Ajith Kumar’s massive net worth, luxury car collection
content: Tamil cinema star Ajith Kumar, known for his racing passion and simple lifestyle, recently marked his comeback with 'Good Bad Ugly'. Born in Secunderabad, his journey began far from films, involving mechanics and motor racing. Despite a net worth of Rs 350 crore and luxury assets, he maintains a grounded persona.

title: 'I saw the threat call': Mamata claims police forced doctors to release Abhishek
content: Trinamool MP Abhishek Banerjee was assaulted and abused by protesters in Sonarpur while visiting the home of a party worker allegedly killed in post-poll violence. Chased, pelted with objects, and his shirt torn, Banerjee blamed BJP workers for a planned attack. He plans to approach higher courts, while the BJP denied involvement and highlighted TMC's past actions against their workers.

title: Minecraft Chaos Cubed update release date, features and more details
content: Mojang has officially announced Minecraft's Chaos Cubed update, set for release on June 16, 2026. This highly anticipated update introduces the new Sulfur Caves biome with unique blocks like Sulfur and Cinnabar, potent sulfur hazards, and the innovative Sulfur Cube mob. Java Edition players will also finally receive a Friends List feature.

title: Two brothers die in lightning strike while trekking to world’s highest Shiva temple
content: Tragedy struck on the Chandrashila trek route near Tungnath. Two brothers from Lucknow were killed by a lightning strike. Their companions survived the severe weather. Rescue teams worked through difficult terrain to recover the bodies. The incident highlights the dangers of high-altitude trekking during bad weather.

title: How has Virat Kohli fared against Gujarat Titans?
content: Virat Kohli's exceptional record against Gujarat Titans, boasting 503 runs at 71.85, continues to be a focal point as Royal Challengers Bengaluru face GT in the IPL 2026 final. Having already surpassed 600 runs this season, Kohli aims to extend his dominance in this crucial encounter.

title: Watch: Massive meteor explosion rattles houses across northeastern US
content: A meteor exploded over the northeastern United States on Saturday, unleashing a sonic boom equivalent to 300 tons of TNT. The bright daytime fireball, estimated to be about three feet wide, fragmented approximately 40 miles above the ground, startling residents across multiple states and causing buildings to shake.

title: Ajith’s rare family reflections: Fans revisit old interviews & motor racing dream
content: Following his mother Mohini Mani's passing, fans are recalling actor Ajith Kumar's rare public discussions about his family. He revealed his mother's Sindhi heritage and his own initial struggles with Tamil due to his mixed background, even admitting to an accent. Ajith also shared how his parents, despite financial constraints, strongly supported his motor racing passion, fostering his dreams.

title: Delhi delivery executive delivers vibrator, asks woman, ‘Why this? Use me instead’
content: Customers are reporting disturbing encounters with delivery personnel who are revealing knowledge of their private purchases, particularly from quick commerce platforms. Incidents involving sexual wellness products highlight a serious breach of customer privacy, raising questions about company protocols and operational pressures leading to these embarrassing and frightening situations.

title: Indian-origin Disney manager gets 2.5-year jail term in Russia over drug case
content: An Indian-origin Disney executive, Jugal Daterao, has received a two-and-a-half-year prison sentence in Russia for drug possession and smuggling.  Authorities found THC-infused gummy candies in his luggage at a Moscow airport. Daterao claimed the gummies were legally prescribed in the US for post-brain surgery effects, but the court rejected his defense.

title: Bengaluru Police issue strict advisory ahead of RCB vs GT IPL 2026 final
content: Bengaluru Police have issued a stern advisory for the IPL 2026 final, urging fans to celebrate responsibly. Public gatherings, unauthorised LED screens, live screenings outside venues, firecrackers, bike rallies, and public intoxication are prohibited. Authorities emphasise cooperation to ensure safety and prevent disorder.

title: Video: Mamata loses her cool at hospital authorities with Abhishek on stretcher
content: A video shows Mamata Banerjee confronting hospital officials in Kolkata, reportedly over her nephew Abhishek Banerjee's treatment after an alleged attack. BJP leaders criticised Banerjee, accusing her of pressuring the hospital despite reports of no major injuries. The incident follows an alleged stone-pelting attack on Abhishek Banerjee during a visit.

title: Ghaziabad 'Bakrid invite' murder: Main accused Asad shot dead in encounter
content: Ghaziabad police have shot dead Asad, the main accused in the fatal stabbing of Class 11 student Surya Chauhan. Asad, who was on the run with a Rs 50,000 bounty, allegedly opened fire on police during an attempt to apprehend him. He succumbed to injuries at the hospital. An injured constable is also undergoing treatment.

title: IPL Final: Kohli vs Siraj, Gill vs Bhuvneshwar - Five key player battles
content: Royal Challengers Bengaluru and Gujarat Titans clash in the IPL 2026 final. Key individual battles, including Sai Sudharsan vs. Jacob Duffy and Shubman Gill vs. Bhuvneshwar Kumar, are poised to significantly influence the outcome of this high-stakes championship match.

title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding: Which 3 guests are off the list?
content: Taylor Swift's wedding guest list reportedly excludes former friend Karlie Kloss due to her association with Scooter Braun.  Blake Lively and Ryan Reynolds are also said to be on the 'no-no' list. Swift is prioritizing guests who have played significant roles in her life, reflecting her desire for lasting connections.

title: Kochi police record statement of Malayalam actor Ansiba Hassan on her complaint against fellow actor, woman cop
content: Police procedure amid an ongoing controversy surrounding AMMA which has been witnessing an internal turmoil with a section of office-bearers making allegations against each other

title: Mekedatu Dam row: Protesting Tamil Nadu farmers stopped at Karnataka border in Krishnagiri
content: The farmers’ association ‘Tamizhaga Cauvery Vivasayigal Sangam’ demands filing of case against Karnataka CM-elect D.K. Shivakumar

title: UDF government in Kerala ‘poised’ to refer ADM Naveen Babu’s suicide case to CBI
content: Officials say government has not yet issued a formal notification of consent authorising the CBI to investigate the case. They say it is likely the government will seek legal opinion and weigh the family’s request in the Cabinet

title: College student dies after getting stuck in temple car wheel in Tiruchengode
content: When the temple car took a sharp left turn during procession, two young men who were closer to the back wheel of the temple car got stuck between the wheel and the wall of the building where the temple car is usually stationed.

title: U.K. Foreign Secretary to visit India, China for talks on global challenges
content: Ms. Cooper is scheduled to hold bilateral talks with External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar on Thursday (June 4), followed by interactions across business and academia

title: Thunderstorm forecast in 21 Telangana districts on June 1
content: Maximum temperatures continued to ease across Telangana on May 30, with no location in the State recording temperatures above 46℃.

title: Three held in J&K’s Uri as Army foils exfiltration bid
content: In a separate incident in the same area, Army personnel intercepted and apprehended a man from Pakistan of Kashmir (PoK) while he was crossing the Line of Control (LoC)

title: Edappadi Palaniswami urges Tamil Nadu Governor to take steps against ‘horse-trading’ by TVK-led government
content: Former Minister ‘Agri’ S.S. Krishnamurthy and Rajya Sabha MP M. Dhanapal submit petition to the Governor

title: ‘This is crucial juncture for space missions’, says Shubhanshu Shukla, as he prepares for second space voyage
content: If the Ganganyaan mission is successful, India will be only the fourth nation in the world to have shown a capability of doing manned space missions, after the U.S., Russia, and China

title: What is changing for UPI and LPG from June 1, 2026? Everything to know
content: Some new changes to the UPI system and the LPG distribution system guidelines will be in effect from June 1, 2026.

title: Police issue advisory on post-IPL 2026 final celebrations in Bengaluru
content: Once the final result is announced, the public should not engage in any kind of celebrations in public places, the police said, adding that maintaining law and order, public peace, and safety is the responsibility of every citizen.

title: Pakistan beaming rogue telecom signals to aid terror networks, penetrate jails in Jammu and Kashmir: Officials
content: Recent infiltration patterns, particularly in the south of the Pir Panjal range, indicate there has been a significant surge in telecom towers erected along LoC in PoK, whose signals reach various Jammu jails where hardcore terrorists are lodged

title: Maratha quota: Jarange ends fast after talks with govt delegation
content: Maratha quota activist Manoj Jarange concluded his latest hunger strike after receiving a comprehensive proposal from Maharashtra government officials

title: No tussle at all with judiciary over appointment of judges: Law Minister
content: Arjun Ram Meghwal explained that appointment systems in other countries are being examined informally and no formal mechanism has been put in place to study them.

title: BJP chief Nitin Nabin on 2-day visit to Jharkhand from June 6
content: BJP chief Nitin Nabin visits Jharkhand on June 6-7 for party events and meetings ahead of upcoming Rajya Sabha elections

title: Jammu and Kashmir CM calls MLAs meet on June 3
content: Jammu and Kashmir Chief Minister Omar Abdullah calls a meeting of MLAs to discuss and deliberate upon the matters of collective importance and issues concerning public welfare

title: Home Minister V. Anitha visits home guard injured in knife attack near Lankelapalem
content: Home Minister V. Anitha meets home guard hurt in an on-duty knife attack and says the accused has been arrested

title: Nara Lokesh to visit Russia from June 2 to attract investments to Andhra Pradesh
content: Information Technology and HRD Minister attends St. Petersburg International Economic Forum, meets industrialists and investors during Russia visit

title: Delhi High Court’s pathbreaking ruling on Google keyword advertising | Explained
content: Justice Mini Pushkarna assessed if a company can buy another’s registered trademark as a keyword on Google Ads to have their ads appear first in searches for that trademark

title: K-TET: teachers’ organisations want Union government to bring in legislative amendment
content: As per a 2025 Supreme Court judgment, in-service teachers are  required to clear Teachers’ Eligibility Test within two years. Recently, court gave an extension of one more year to pass the test

title: South Coast Railway zone in Visakhapatnam goes operational under GM Sandeep Mathur
content: South Coast Railway, India’s 18th railway zone, becomes operational from June 1 under General Manager Sandeep Mathur

title: Kerala-based group to launch Community Marine Biodiversity Online Register on World Oceans Day
content: Created by Friends of Marine Life, the online register documents marine ecosystems and coastal biodiversity off the coast of Thiruvananthapuram district in Kerala

title: Second-life surgery | Surrendered Maoists in Maharashtra reverse vasectomies for family life
content: As men in Maharashtra’s Maoist-affected Gadchiroli district give up arms, their outlook on life has changed. This report discusses how they are reversing the vasectomies that a life of resistance in the forest had forced them into

title: Bengaluru East Corporation seizes six vehicles for illegal dumping of waste along riverbank, FIR registered
content: Using fake identity cards, they allegedly collected waste and dumped it illegally in areas adjoining the Dakshina Pinakini river near Siddhartha Layout in the Kadugodi jurisdiction

title: The Sulur horror story of child sexual assault and murder
content: The shocking sexual assault and murder of a 10-year-old girl near Sulur in Coimbatore comes as a reminder that constant vigilance and creating a safe society for women and girls should be among the top priorities for the new government in Tamil Nadu

title: Donald Trump's daughter visits Jaisalmer, security heightened
content: The couple reached Jaisalmer Civil Airport from Delhi on a special aircraft amid tight security arrangements

title: Discussions underway to appoint new KPCC chief, says Kharge
content: The Leader of Opposition in Rajya Sabha addressed key queries regarding the formation of new council of Ministers and the appointment of a new State unit president, and rumours surrounding multiple Deputy Chief Minister (DCM) posts

title: Telangana CM Revanth to review the rollout of Employee Health Scheme
content: Chief Minister A. Revanth Reddy is expected to conduct a review meeting with senior officials on the modalities of implementing the scheme on June 1

title: Revanth Reddy to embark on multi-district tour, launch key development projects across Telangana from June 1
content: The five-day tour is expected to provide fresh momentum to several flagship programmes and infrastructure projects

title: Analysing the link between adaptability and species success of Elvira rat, Cutch rock rat
content: R. Brawin Kumar, a wildlife biologist, told the rock rats are primary food sources for species such as the Bengal fox. “Their protection and presence in these landscapes is crucial for the local rocky ecosystems they inhabit,” he said.

title: TMC MP Kalyan Banerjee claims BJP supporters attacked, injured him outside police station
content: Tension prevailed outside the Chanditala police station as a group of people waved black flags at Kalyan Banerjee, the MP from Hoogly district's Sreerampore, and shouted "thief" at him and other TMC delegation

title: Estonian startups keen to partner India, tap into its huge market
content: Latitude59, an influential startup and technology conference that takes place here annually, is also keen on hosting a networking event in India next year under its aegis

title: AIADMK raises concerns over T.N. Minister Aadhav Arjuna holding two posts
content: Tamil Nadu Minister for Sports Development Aadhav Arjuna holds two posts — one as a regulator and another as an administrator — at the same time

title: POCSO Act applies if victim of commercial sex trafficking is minor: Supreme Court
content: The Bench said a person's awareness that they are being employed in the sex industry or prostitution does not exclude them from being a victim of trafficking, as they may have been deceived about the conditions of work, which later turned out to be exploitative

title: Blue moon 2026: will the ‘micromoon’ be visible in India?
content: The rare blue boon returns after nearly three years, coinciding with a micromoon. Find out when it will be visible in India

title: IAS officers reshuffle in Tamil Nadu: Five new Collectors, three Corporation Commissioners appointed
content: New Collectors posted in Perambalur, Ariyalur, Chennai, Tiruvallur and Ramanathapuram districts. Coimbatore, Tiruchi and Madurai get new Corporation Commissioners.

title: The end of Waltair: How Visakhapatnam got its own railway division
content: The railway network in this region dates to 1893, when broad-gauge operations began as part of the East Coast Railways

title: Tamil Nadu CM Vijay calls on PM Modi at New Delhi; discusses Mekedatu, fishermen issues
content: Mr. Vijay urged the Prime Minister to direct the Union Home Ministry to issue a clarification permitting the respective State invocation songs to be sung at the beginning of all government events.

title: Mallikarjun Kharge meets Telangana leaders during Hyderabad stopover
content: The meeting assumes significance in the backdrop of rumours that Mr. Goud is pushing for a Cabinet berth in the next expansion

title: BJP alleges Mamata pressured private hospital to admit Abhishek Banerjee
content: Making the allegation in an X post, West Bengal BJP spokesperson Debjit Sarkar shared an audio clip in which Mamata Banerjee was purportedly heard expressing anger over the hospital's reluctance to admit the Diamond Harbour MP

title: D.K. Shivakumar | ‘Rock’ of Congress
content: The party’s crisis manager, known for his organisational skills and grassroots networks, is set to become Karnataka’s Chief Minister

title: Power Minister pushes for timely settlement of West Bengal State-depts’ dues, shift to prepaid metering
content: The West Bengal government committed to saturate smart meters across government establishments by June this year, and transitioning to prepaid by August

title: Sitting VC on search panel raises conflict of interest concerns, says expert
content: The Department of Agriculture, through a notification dated May 14, constituted a search committee to recommend a panel of eligible candidates for the post of Vice-Chancellor of KSNUAHS, Shivamogga

title: WhatsApp, training and tenecteplase injection bring heart attack care to rural Punjab
content: By combining WhatsApp-based coordination, training for frontline healthcare workers, and the clot-busting injection tenecteplase, doctors are improving access to timely heart attack care in rural Punjab

title: Manoj Bajpayee, Huma Qureshi, Kritika Kamra to headline Hindu Huddle sessions in Bengaluru
content: Actors will discuss their journeys, roles, evolving craft, and changing landscape of Indian entertainment

title: Candid conversations on Kashmir expected at The Hindu Huddle
content: J&K CM Omar Abdullah will be engaged in candid dialogue with Dr. Narayan Lakshman, Opinion Editor, The Hindu, and Curator, The Hindu Huddle, in a session titled ‘Beyond the Valley: The role of Kashmir in great power politics’ on June 5

title: BJP will form the government again in 2027 under Dhami’s leadership in Uttarakhand: Nitin Nabin
content: Speaking at a gathering in Dehradun, Nabin highlighted the Dhami government’s impactful initiatives, including the implementation of the Uniform Civil Code and successful management of the Char Dham Yatra.

title: Mann Ki Baat: PM Modi urges people to take precautions amid scorching heat
content: In his monthly radio broadcast Mann Ki Baat, he said people should take extra precautions when stepping out in the sun

title: Chamba accident: Operation under way to retrieve bodies from gorge
content: The accident occurred when the car was going towards the snow-bound Sach Pass, one of the toughest stretches in Himachal Pradesh, the officials said

title: Watch: Mamata slams ‘barbaric’ attack on Abhishek Banerjee, says TMC won’t be intimidated
content: Trinamool Congress chief Mamata Banerjee has strongly condemned the attack on Abhishek Banerjee during his visit to a violence-hit party worker’s family in Sonarpur.

title: Five arrested for attack on Abhishek, Mamata says police pressurised hospitals not to admit him
content: After a mob attacked him at Sonarpur, the Trinamool Congress general secretary was taken to two private hospitals in Kolkata and both the institutions refused admission to him on grounds that the injuries were not serious in nature.

title: BJP Core Group Meet At PM Modi's Residence Today, Rajya Sabha Polls On Agenda
content: Senior Union ministers including Amit Shah, Rajnath Singh, JP Nadda, party president Nitin Nabin and party's top brass are set to gather at PM Modi's residence.

title: Chinese Missile Likely Downed US F-15 Fighter Jet In Iran: Report
content: US officials said the equipment could have improved Iran’s ability to track advanced fighter jets like the US F-15E Strike Eagle.

title: Narrower Than A Fridge, Deadlier Than Thai Caves: Inside Laos Cave Rescue
content: The questions outnumbered solutions. Were the miners safe, or even alive? Could they be reached? How would they use regulators, gauges and oxygen tanks, diving equipment they probably would have never seen, let alone used?

title: On Camera, Trinamool MP Attacked, Day After Abhishek Banerjee Assaulted
content: Kalyan Banerjee was greeted with black flags and 'chor chor' slogans, similar to the public outrage that Abhishek Banerjee faced a day earlier.

title: Israel Captures Strategic Castle In Lebanon In Deepest Incursion Since 26 Years
content: The capture of Beaufort castle near the city of Nabatiyeh came after days of intense fighting and airstrikes in nearby villages where Israeli troops fought Hezbollah members in the rugged area.

title: Horses Bolt Through Rome After Policeman Lights Fireworks At Parade Rehearsal
content: Fireworks set off without permission by a traffic policeman during a latenight rehearsal for Italy's Republic Day parade startled dozens of horses, sending them bolting

title: Indian Man In Canada Claims He Wasn't Aware His Friend Was In Lawrence Bishnoi Gang
content: Canadian immigration authorities plan to deport Jashandeep Singh over his links to Arshdeep Dalla and a shooting incident last August.

title: A 2022 Volcanic Eruption May Hold Clue To Cut Gas Behind Global Warming: Report
content: The volcano erupted in January 2022 with a force hundreds of times greater than the Hiroshima nuclear explosion, sending ash, steam and gas nearly 40 miles into the sky

title: Poll Body Begins Electoral Roll Revision In Manipur, Final List By September
content: The final Electoral Roll will be published on September 6 after all claims and objections have been duly considered and disposed of.

title: Munna Jhingada: Dawood Ibrahim's 'Right Hand' At Centre Of Planning Attacks In India
content: Munna Jhingada, alias Syed Mudassar Hussain, is a dreaded shooter accused of planning a major terrorist attack in India.

title: POCSO Applies If Minor Trafficked For Commercial Sexual Exploitation: Supreme Court
content: A bench of Justices issued a slew of directions to mitigate the concerns of sex workers, while clarifying the legal framework for the prosecution of perpetrators and the rehabilitation of victims.

title: How Pakistan's Social Media Star Shahzad Bhatti Became ISI's New Weapon
content: On Saturday, the Delhi Police Special Cell arrested four suspects linked to Shahzad Bhatti's module, who were allegedly planning major attacks across the country.

title: "Very Interesting": Trump Says Iran Has Agreed To No Nuclear Weapons
content: Trump has said his priorities for any deal include stopping Iran from any nuclear weapon development and re-opening the blockaded Strait of Hormuz.

title: Centre Halves Petrol Export Tax, Cuts Diesel, Aviation Fuel Levy From June
content: The finance ministry in a notification said road and infrastructure cess will be nil on export of petrol and diesel.

title: "You Did Wrong": Mamata Banerjee Heard Scolding Hospital CEO, BJP Shares Video
content: A state BJP leader has shared a clip in which Banerjee was allegedly heard yelling at Pradeep Tandon, CEO of the Belle Vue Hospital in Kolkata.

title: Himanta Sarma To Expand Cabinet On June 5, 9 MLAs Likely To Be Inducted
content: The BJP-led National Democratic Alliance (NDA) returned to power for a third consecutive term in the state, winning 102 out of 126 seats in the April 9 elections

title: 5 Arrested For Attack On Trinamool Leader Abhishek Banerjee In Bengal
content: Banerjee was attacked when he went to visit the victims of post-poll violence, and later claimed that BJP activists were trying to kill him. Unidentified people scuffled with the leader and roughed him up while raising chants of "thief, thief".

title: 'Yet another propaganda ploy': Kyiv denies Russian claim of drone strike on nuclear plant
content: At TOI World Desk, our dedicated team of seasoned journalists and passionate writers tirelessly sifts through the vast tapestry of global events to bring you the latest news and diverse perspectives round the clock. title: End of an era for Air India colonies in Mumbai’s Kalina as last of the occupants leave
content: the first staff colony was followed by three more through the 198th century . the colony is the first of the four constructed way back in 1955 . on Sunday, the last of the occupants will leave, and Mumbai International Airport Ltd will take possession of the land leased to it two decades ago .

title: 'We stand united': Abhishek on Rahul's support after attack, vows to continue the fight against BJP
content: The TOI News Desk comprises a dedicated and tireless team of journalists who operate around the clock to deliver the most current and comprehensive news and updates to the readers of The Times of India worldwide.
